title: Cordiera claviflora
taxonomy: Cordiera claviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cordiera, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes many species of plants with a wide range of characteristics and growth habits. The classification of Cordiera claviflora reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Cordiera claviflora, also known as Cordiera, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cordiera claviflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cordiera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of capsules or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies and supplements.
